title: 10 Ways To Use Instagram Stories for Business Growth
description: If you’re trying to grow your brand on Instagram, posting Instagram Reels and feed posts is only part of the strategy. One of the most powerful tools you…
excerpt: If you’re trying to grow your brand on Instagram, posting Instagram Reels and feed posts is only part of the strategy. One of the most powerful tools you have is often overlooked. That tool is…
author: Daniel R.
heroAlt: 10 Ways To Use Instagram Stories for Business Growth — Social Media guide on Thunderclap

blocks.html:
If you’re trying to grow your brand on Instagram, posting Instagram Reels and feed posts is only part of the strategy. One of the most powerful tools you have is often overlooked. That tool is Instagram Stories.

Using Instagram Stories for business is one of the easiest ways to build trust, stay top of mind, and turn followers into customers. 

It’s not a secret that stories feel more personal, more real, and more immediate than regular posts. It’s an incredible way to connect with your IG community and build your account. 

The best part is you do not need perfect content. You just need to show up consistently and give people a reason to engage.

Let’s break down how Stories work and how you can use them to grow your business.

What Is Instagram Stories?

Instagram Stories are short pieces of content that disappear after 24 hours. They can include photos, videos, text, stickers, links, and more.

Stories show up at the top of the Instagram app, which makes them one of the first things users see when they log in.

Unlike feed posts, Stories are designed for quick, casual content. You can share updates, behind the scenes moments, and interactive posts without worrying about perfection.

For businesses, this is a huge advantage. You can connect with your audience daily without needing polished content every time.

You can also use Instagram hashtags in stories to appear in searches and story feeds, which expands your reach and algorithm performance. 

Stories Has Changed The Way We Use Instagram

Instagram used to be all about curated photos and perfect feeds. Now, it is more about real time content and connection.

Stories have played a big role in that shift.

People now use Instagram to check in on their favorite creators and brands throughout the day. They want to see what you are doing, what you are thinking, and what is happening behind the scenes.

Stories make your brand feel more human. They give your audience a reason to keep coming back.

Instead of posting once and disappearing, Stories allow you to stay visible all day long.

Why Should You Be Using Instagram Stories for Business?

If you are not using Stories, you are missing one of the best ways to build relationships with your audience.

Stories help you stay consistent, even when you do not have new feed posts or Reels to share.

Build trust and authenticity

Stories feel more real than polished posts. This makes it easier for your audience to trust you.

When people see your daily updates, they feel like they know you. 

That trust and audience connection makes them more likely to support your business.

Increase engagement and interaction

Stories are packed with features that encourage interaction. Polls, questions, quizzes, and sliders make it easy for people to engage with your content.

This kind of interaction sends strong signals to Instagram that your content is valuable.

Drive traffic and sales

Stories are great for promoting products, services, and offers.

You can add links, share testimonials, and guide your audience to take action. Because Stories feel less formal, people are often more open to clicking and exploring.

You can also link products to Instagram stories, allowing users to buy from within the IG story interface. 

If you’re looking to boost sales and grow your Instagram, this is a feature that you can’t miss! 

How Should You Use Stories Within Your Business?

The key to using Instagram Stories for business is to be intentional.

You do not want to post random content with no purpose. Instead, think about how each story fits into your overall strategy.

Ask yourself what you want your audience to do. Do you want them to engage, learn, or take action?

Once you know your goal, it becomes much easier to create content that works.

1. Share behind the scenes content

People are naturally curious. They want to see what goes on behind your brand, not just the final result.

Behind the scenes content helps you feel more real and approachable. This could be anything from showing your workspace, packing orders, planning content, or even your daily routine as a business owner.

For example, if you run an online shop, you can show how you prepare orders, check inventory, or design new products. If you are a service provider, you can show how you plan client work or brainstorm ideas.

This type of content builds trust because it removes the “perfect” image and replaces it with something authentic. When people feel like they know you, they are more likely to support your business.

It also gives you easy content ideas. You do not always need to create something new. Just document what you are already doing.

2. Use polls and question stickers

If you want to boost engagement, this is one of the easiest ways to do it.

Polls and question stickers give your audience a simple way to interact with you without much effort. Instead of just watching, they can tap, respond, and feel involved. Here’s a great example of an Instagram story poll: 

You can use polls to ask opinions about products, content ideas, or even simple preferences. 

For example, “Which design do you like better?” or “What topic should I cover next?”

Question stickers go a step further. They allow your audience to ask questions, share thoughts, or give feedback.

This can give you valuable insights into what your audience actually wants.

The more people interact with your Stories, the more Instagram shows your content to them. This helps keep your brand top of mind.

3. Promote your products or services

Stories are one of the best places to promote what you offer without feeling overly sales focused.

Instead of posting a single sales message, you can break it into multiple Story frames. This allows you to explain your offer in a more natural and engaging way.

Start by highlighting a problem your audience faces. Then show how your product or service solves that problem. Finally, guide them to take action, such as clicking a link or sending a message.

You can also use features like product tags or link stickers to make it easy for people to learn more.

The key is to make your promotion feel helpful, not pushy. Focus on benefits and real use cases instead of just listing features.

4. Share testimonials and social proof

People trust other people more than they trust brands. That is why social proof is so powerful.

When you share testimonials in your Stories, you are showing real results from real customers. This helps remove doubt and builds confidence in your business.

You can share screenshots of reviews, before and after results, or even video testimonials if you have them. SoCal French Bulldog Rescue often shows success stories and happy testimonials of rescue dogs with their fosters or new owners to inspire confidence in other adopters or fosters: 

Try to add context when you share these. For example, explain what the customer was struggling with and how your product or service helped them.

You can also highlight these Stories so new visitors can see them anytime. This turns your social proof into a long term asset.

5. Educate your audience

Educational content is one of the best ways to keep your audience engaged and coming back.

When you teach something useful, people see you as a trusted source. This builds authority and makes your brand more valuable in their eyes.

You can break down tips into short Story slides. For example, share one idea per slide to keep it simple and easy to follow.

If you are in marketing, you can share quick Instagram growth tips. If you are in fitness, you can share simple workout advice. If you run a business, you can share lessons or insights.

Educational content also performs well because people are more likely to save it or come back to it later.

6. Build anticipation for launches

If you have something coming up, Stories are the perfect place to build excitement.

Instead of announcing everything at once, you can create a buildup over several days. This keeps your audience curious and engaged.

Start by teasing your upcoming launch. Share small hints, previews, or behind the scenes clips. Then gradually reveal more details. Target did this recently with an invite to the exclusive Pokemon collection: 

You can also use countdown stickers to remind people when your launch is happening. This creates a sense of urgency and encourages people to take action.

The more anticipation you build, the more attention your launch will get.

7. Go live and share real time updates

Going live is one of the most direct ways to connect with your audience.

It allows you to have real conversations, answer questions, and share your thoughts in real time. This level of interaction builds strong relationships.

You can use live sessions for Q and A, product demos, tutorials, or even casual chats with your audience.

If going live feels intimidating, start small. You can do short sessions or plan simple topics to talk about.

After your IG live ends, you can share parts of it in your Stories so more people can see it.

Real time content makes your brand feel active and engaged.

8. Repost user generated content

User generated content is one of the most powerful forms of Instagram marketing.

When your audience shares content about your brand, it acts as a recommendation. This is more convincing than anything you can say yourself.

Reposting this content in your Stories shows appreciation and encourages others to do the same.

For example, if a customer tags your product in their post, you can reshare it and add a short message. This makes your audience feel valued and part of your community. Check out this example below from ThirstyGirl coffee: 

It also gives you a steady stream of content without needing to create everything yourself.

Over time, this builds a stronger connection between your brand and your audience.

Get More Instagram Followers

Using Instagram Stories for business can also help you grow your audience.

When people engage with your Stories, it increases your visibility. Instagram is more likely to show your content to those users again.

Stories also help turn casual viewers into loyal followers.

If someone watches your Stories regularly, they are more likely to follow you, engage with your posts, and eventually become a customer.

Focus on creating content that keeps people coming back every day.

You can also buy Instagram followers with Thunderclap to gain traction and increase your performance against the algorithm, helping promote more story views and more movement on your Instagram! 

Final Thoughts: Instagram Stories for Business

Instagram Stories are one of the most powerful tools you can use to grow your business online.

They help you stay visible, build trust, and connect with your audience in a real way.

You do not need perfect content. You just need to show up consistently and provide value.

If you start using Stories with intention, you will see a difference in your engagement and your overall growth.

Keep it simple, stay consistent, and focus on your audience.

FAQs

How often should businesses post Instagram Stories?

Most businesses should aim to post Stories daily. Even a few updates per day can help you stay visible and keep your audience engaged.

Do Instagram Stories help grow your business?

Yes, Stories help build relationships, increase engagement, and drive traffic. Over time, this can lead to more followers and more sales.

What type of content works best for Instagram Stories?

Behind the scenes content, educational tips, interactive posts, and product highlights all perform well. The key is to keep it simple and engaging.

Can Instagram Stories increase sales?

Yes, Stories can drive sales by showcasing your products, sharing testimonials, and adding direct links. They make it easy for your audience to take action quickly.

title: 9 Tested Tips to Boost Instagram engagement in 2026
description: If your Instagram posts are getting views but not much interaction, you are not alone. Many creators and businesses are struggling with the same thing…
excerpt: If your Instagram posts are getting views but not much interaction, you are not alone. Many creators and businesses are struggling with the same thing right now. The platform has changed, and what…
author: Samuel B. Gillham
heroAlt: 9 Tested Tips to Boost Instagram engagement in 2026 — Social Media guide on Thunderclap

blocks.html:
If your Instagram posts are getting views but not much interaction, you are not alone. Many creators and businesses are struggling with the same thing right now. 

The platform has changed, and what worked a few years ago does not work the same today.

If you want to boost Instagram engagement, you need to focus on what actually makes people stop, interact, and care about your content.

Engagement is not just about likes anymore. It includes comments, shares, saves, replies, and even how long people spend on your posts. 

The more people interact with your content, the more Instagram will push it out to others. That’s how the Instagram algorithm works. 

This guide will walk you through proven tips to help you grow your engagement in 2026 and build a stronger presence on Instagram! 

Let’s go. 

Key takeaways

  • Engagement matters more than follower count for growth
  • Content quality and relevance drive most interactions
  • Services like Thunderclap can help boost numbers
  • Video and Reels are key for reach and engagement
  • Consistency and community interaction build long term results

What is a good engagement rate on Instagram?

A good engagement rate depends on your niche, audience size, and content type. But in general, most accounts fall into a few ranges.

For smaller accounts, an engagement rate between 3 percent and 6 percent is considered strong. Mid sized accounts often see around 2 percent to 4 percent. Larger accounts may have lower rates because their audience is broader.

Instead of comparing yourself to others, focus on improving your own numbers over time.

Engagement rate is usually calculated by taking total interactions like likes, comments, shares, and saves, and dividing that by your number of followers.

But here is what really matters. Are people actually responding to your content? Are they saving it, sharing it, or starting conversations?

That is the kind of engagement that helps you grow.

How to increase your engagement on Instagram

If you want to boost Instagram engagement, you need a mix of strong content, smart Instagram marketing strategy, and real interaction.

Let’s go through the tips that actually work.

1. Track your Instagram engagement

You cannot improve what you do not measure.

Start by looking at your Instagram insights. Pay attention to which posts get the most saves, shares, and comments.

Notice patterns. Do your educational posts perform better than your personal ones? Do your videos get more engagement than your photos?

Tracking helps you understand what your audience wants.

Over time, you can double down on what works and stop wasting time on what does not. You can also use Instagram monitoring tools to help you track things if you need more guidance. 

Once you understand where your engagement is, you can track how it performs over time. 

Using a service like Thunderclap can help you get a quick boost so that you have strong numbers as you maintain your content strategy. 

You can buy Instagram likes, comments, views, and more with top-quality Thunderclap services

2. Create valuable content

If your content is not valuable, people will scroll past it.

Value can mean different things depending on your niche. It can be educational, entertaining, inspiring, or relatable.

Ask yourself one question before posting. Why would someone care about this?

If your content helps solve a problem, teaches something useful, or makes someone feel something, it is much more likely to get engagement.

Your Instagram captions are also important in hooking people in and providing them with the deeper story or value point. 

The more value you provide, the more people will interact with your posts.

3. Master your aesthetic

Your Instagram profile is often the first impression people get of your brand.

A clean and consistent look helps build trust and keeps people interested in your content.

This does not mean everything has to look perfect. It just needs to feel cohesive.

Use similar colors, fonts, and styles so your posts feel connected. When someone visits your profile, they should instantly understand your vibe and what you are about.

A strong aesthetic can increase engagement because people are more likely to follow and interact with content that looks polished and clear.

4. Embrace video content

Video content is one of the fastest ways to boost Instagram engagement right now.

Reels, in particular, are designed for reach. Instagram pushes them out to new audiences based on how engaging they are.

Video allows you to connect with your audience in a more dynamic way. You can show personality, explain ideas, and tell stories more effectively.

You do not need fancy equipment! Clear visuals, good lighting, and strong messaging are enough.

Focus on grabbing attention in the first few seconds and keeping viewers interested until the end.

5. Engage with Instagram users

Engagement is a two way street.

If you want people to interact with your content, you need to interact with theirs too.

Reply to comments on your posts. Respond to direct messages. Like and comment on posts from your audience and people in your niche.

This builds relationships and makes your account more visible.

When people feel noticed, they are more likely to support your content and engage with you regularly.

6. Use hashtag and location tags

Hashtags and location tags still help with discovery, even if they are not as powerful as they once were.

They help Instagram understand your content and show it to the right audience.

Use a mix of niche and relevant hashtags that match your content. Avoid using random or overly broad hashtags that do not connect to your post.

Location tags can also help you reach people in a specific area, which is especially useful for local businesses.

Think of hashtags and tags as support tools. They help your content reach the right people, but they do not replace good content.

7. Get creative with Instagram Stories and Reels

Stories and Reels are powerful tools for engagement because they offer different ways to connect with your audience.

Stories are great for real time interaction. You can use polls, questions, and stickers to encourage people to engage directly with you.

Reels help you reach new audiences and bring more people into your content.

Using both together creates a strong strategy. Reels bring people in, and Stories help build relationships.

The more ways you give people to interact with you, the higher your engagement will be.

8. Produce consistent quality content

Consistency is key if you want to boost Instagram engagement.

Posting once in a while will not get you far. You need to show up regularly so your audience stays connected to your content.

At the same time, quality matters. Posting often without value will not help.

Find a balance that works for you. It is better to post fewer high quality posts than many low quality ones.

Consistency builds trust and keeps your audience engaged over time.

9. Post at the optimum time of day for engagement

Timing can affect how your content performs.

Posting when your audience is most active increases the chances that people will see and interact with your content.

Check your Instagram insights to find out when your followers are online.

While timing is not everything, it can give your posts a stronger start. Early engagement signals to Instagram that your content is worth pushing to more people.

Final Thoughts to Boost your Instagram Engagement

If you want to boost Instagram engagement in 2026, you need to focus on what actually matters.

It is not about tricks or shortcuts. It is about creating content that people care about and building real connections with your audience.

When you combine strong content, consistency, and genuine interaction, your engagement will grow naturally.

Keep testing, keep learning, and keep showing up. Over time, your results will follow.

FAQs

Still have some questions? Let’s take a look at the common questions we get. 

What is the fastest way to boost Instagram engagement?

The fastest way is to create engaging video content, especially Reels, and focus on strong hooks that grab attention right away. Pair that with responding to comments and messages to build interaction quickly.

Why is my Instagram engagement so low?

Low engagement usually comes from content that is not connecting with your audience. It could be unclear messaging, weak hooks, or inconsistent posting. Reviewing your insights can help you identify the issue.

Do hashtags still help boost Instagram engagement?

Yes, hashtags still help, but they are not the main driver of engagement. They support discovery, while your content quality and relevance play a bigger role.

How often should I post to increase engagement?

Posting consistently is more important than posting frequently. For most creators and businesses, posting three to five times per week is a good starting point, as long as the content is high quality.

title: 5 Best Instagram Reels Tips to Grow Your Instagram
description: If you’re trying to grow on Instagram right now, Reels are not optional. They are the main way to get reach, attract new followers, and build your brand…
excerpt: If you’re trying to grow on Instagram right now, Reels are not optional. They are the main way to get reach, attract new followers, and build your brand. But here’s the problem. A lot of creators are…
author: Rebecca Adley
heroAlt: 5 Best Instagram Reels Tips to Grow Your Instagram — Social Media guide on Thunderclap

blocks.html:
If you’re trying to grow on Instagram right now, Reels are not optional. They are the main way to get reach, attract new followers, and build your brand.

But here’s the problem. A lot of creators are posting Reels and still not seeing results. Low views, low engagement, and no real growth.

That usually comes down to your Instagram marketing strategy and how you create your content. 

This guide breaks down the most effective Instagram Reels tips so you can actually get traction. 

Whether you’re a creator, small business owner, or brand, these tips will help you grow your 

audience and improve your content.

Let’s do it! 

What Are Instagram Reels?

Instagram Reels are short form videos that can be up to 90 seconds long. They are designed to entertain, educate, or inspire users in a quick and engaging way.

Instagram Reels show up in multiple places on the app. They appear in the Reels feed, the Explore page, and sometimes even in the main home feed. That gives your content more chances to be discovered by people who don’t follow you yet.

Unlike regular posts, Reels are built for reach. Instagram pushes them out to new audiences based on how engaging they are.

This is why Reels are one of the most powerful tools for growth right now. Let’s talk more about why they matter if you want to build a strong Instagram perforance. 

Why You Need Instagram Reels Tips

Posting random videos and hoping they perform is not a strategy. Instagram is competitive, and the algorithm rewards content that keeps people watching.

If your Reels don’t hook viewers in the first few seconds or hold attention, they will not get pushed out.

That’s why having clear Instagram Reels tips matters. You need to understand what works so you can create content that performs.

Good Reels can help you:

  • Reach people who have never heard of you
  • Grow your followers faster
  • Build trust with your audience
  • Drive traffic to your offers or services

Without a strategy, you are just guessing. But, with the right approach, you can turn Reels into your main growth engine.

Best Instagram Reels Tips for IG Growth

Let’s get into the tips that actually make a difference and can transform the way you approach your IG content strategy. 

Tip 1: Use trending audios on your Reels when you need a sound or music

Using trending audio on Instagram can give your Reel a boost, but only if it makes sense for your content.

Instagram often pushes content that uses popular sounds because those sounds are already gaining traction. 

This means your Reel has a better chance of being shown to more people.

The mistake many people make is forcing a trending audio that does not match their message. That usually leads to low watch time and poor performance.

Instead, look for trending sounds that naturally fit your content. If you are sharing tips, storytelling, or behind the scenes content, choose audio that supports that tone.

Also, pay attention to timing. Trends move fast. If you use a sound weeks after it peaks, it will not help much.

A simple way to spot trends is to scroll your Reels feed and look for the same audio showing up repeatedly.

Tip 2: Be aware of common pitfalls when adding text to your Reels

Text is one of the most important parts of your Reel because it helps grab attention and keep people watching.

But there are a few common mistakes that can hurt your reach.

One big issue is placing text too close to the edges. Instagram’s interface can cover parts of your video with buttons, captions, or icons. If your text is hidden, viewers miss your message.

Another mistake is using too much text. When viewers feel overwhelmed, they scroll away. For that reason, you need to keep your message clear and easy to read.

Also, your first line of text should act as a hook. This is what stops people from scrolling. For example, starting with something like “3 mistakes small business owners make on Instagram” gives people a reason to stay.

Think of your text as a guide: it should lead the viewer through your content without distracting them.

You can use emojis on Instagram, but it’s better to keep them in the caption and not in your cover text. 

Tip 3: Upload a cover image to your Reel

Your cover image is what people see when they visit your profile. It plays a big role in whether someone clicks on your content.

If you rely on random frames from your video, your profile can look messy and unorganized. That can turn people away.

Instead, choose or design a cover that clearly shows what your Reel is about. Use bold text and simple visuals so people can quickly understand the topic.

A clean and consistent set of covers also makes your profile look more professional, which builds trust and makes people more likely to follow you.

Think of your profile like a storefront. What do people see as they pass by? Your covers are what will attract people and keep them coming back. 

You should also make sure that you use the correct image sizes and video sizes when you create reels. 

Tip 4: Save your reels and save as drafts before you share

This tip is simple but often overlooked.

Saving your Reel before posting gives you a backup. This is helpful in case something goes wrong or you want to reuse the content later.

Saving as a draft also gives you time to review your content. You can check for mistakes, improve your caption, or adjust your hashtags and keywords.

Sometimes the difference between a good Reel and a great one is a small tweak. Taking a few extra minutes to review can improve your results.

It also helps you stay consistent. You can batch create content and schedule your posts instead of rushing to post every day.

Tip 5: Don’t forget to add ‘topics’ to your Reel settings

Instagram now allows you to add topics to your Reels. This is an important feature that many people ignore.

Topics help Instagram understand what your content is about. This makes it easier for the algorithm to show your Reel to the right audience.

For example, if your content is about fitness, choosing the right topic increases the chances that people interested in fitness will see your video.

This is similar to using keywords. The clearer you are about your content, the better Instagram can distribute it.

It only takes a few seconds to select topics, but it can make a difference in your reach.

BONUS TIP: Share your reels to your Instagram story

Sharing your Reel to your story is a simple way to boost visibility.

Not all of your followers will see your Reel in their feed. Posting it to your story gives it another chance to be seen.

You can also add stickers, text, or a call to action to encourage people to watch. For example, you can say “New Reel just posted” or “Tap to watch.”

This can increase engagement in the first few hours, which is important for performance.

Early engagement signals to Instagram that your content is worth pushing to more people.

Content Best Practices for Instagram

Even with the best Instagram Reels tips, your content still needs to be strong.

You can use a service like Thunderclap to buy Instagram followers, likes, comments, and more, which is a great way to boost your overall performance. 

Still, you need to have a long-term content plan in place so that you can see success continue to unfold. 

Here are a few key practices to keep in mind for Instagram content creation: 

First, focus on the hook. The first three seconds of your Reel are critical because if you don’t grab attention quickly, people will scroll away.

Second, keep your content clear and focused. Each Reel should have one main idea. Trying to cover too much can confuse viewers.

Third, aim for high watch time. The longer people watch your video, the better it performs. This means you should keep your content engaging from start to finish.

Fourth, use captions and keywords. Instagram now reads your captions to understand your content. This helps with search and discovery.

Fifth, be consistent. Growth on Instagram takes time. Posting regularly helps you stay visible and improve your skills.

Final Thoughts: Instagram Reels Tips

Reels are one of the best ways to grow on Instagram right now, but success does not happen by accident. Simply posting any content as a reel will not drive growth. 

You need to be intentional with your content, from the audio you choose to the way you present your message! 

The good news is you do not need to be perfect, and you just need to be consistent and willing to learn what works.

How can you do that? Focus on creating content that people enjoy, find useful, or relate to. When you do that, growth becomes much easier.

These Instagram Reels tips will guide you, allowing you to test different approaches and keep improving over time.

FAQs

What is the 5-3-1 rule on Instagram?

The 5-3-1 rule is a simple engagement strategy. It suggests that you should interact with five posts, leave three meaningful comments, and send one direct message each day.

This helps you build relationships and increase visibility, which can support your overall growth.

How do you make a good Instagram reel?

A good Reel starts with a strong hook, delivers value or entertainment, and keeps viewers watching until the end.

It should be clear, engaging, and relevant to your audience. Using good lighting, clean visuals, and clear audio also helps improve quality.

What are common mistakes to avoid in reels?

Some common mistakes include weak hooks, too much text, poor video quality, and using irrelevant trends.

Another big mistake is not understanding your audience. If your content does not connect with viewers, it will not perform well.

What is the 3 second rule on Instagram?

The 3 second rule means you need to grab attention within the first three seconds of your Reel.

If you do not hook the viewer quickly, they will scroll away. This is why your opening line, visuals, and energy are so important.

title: Are Instagram Hashtags Dead? Answer + 5 Tips for Getting Reach
description: If you’ve been posting on Instagram lately and your reach feels… off, you’re not alone. A lot of creators and business owners are asking the same…
excerpt: If you’ve been posting on Instagram lately and your reach feels… off, you’re not alone. A lot of creators and business owners are asking the same question: are Instagram hashtags dead? Short answer…
author: Selina Katoozian
heroAlt: Are Instagram Hashtags Dead? Answer + 5 Tips for Getting Reach — Social Media guide on Thunderclap

blocks.html:
If you’ve been posting on Instagram lately and your reach feels… off, you’re not alone. 

 

A lot of creators and business owners are asking the same question: are Instagram hashtags dead?

 

Short answer: no, but they’re not what they used to be.

 

Instagram has changed a lot over the past few years. The algorithm now focuses more on content quality, keywords, and user behavior than just hashtags alone. 

 

That means hashtags aren’t your main growth tool anymore, but they still have a place if you use them the right way.

 

Let’s break it down so you know exactly how to get reach in 2026.

What is a Hashtag and How do They Work?

A hashtag is a word or phrase with a “#” in front of it, like #socialmediamarketing or #smallbusinessowner. See the cute example with blue hashtagsbelow: 

 

On Instagram, hashtags help categorize content so people can discover posts based on topics they’re interested in.

 

Back in the day, hashtags were one of the main ways to grow. You could add 30 hashtags, hit “post,” and your content could show up in hashtag feeds and explode in reach.

 

Now, Instagram uses hashtags more as a support signal, not the main driver.

 

Here’s how hashtags work today:

  • They help Instagram understand what your content is about
  • They can still place your post in smaller niche discovery feeds
  • They act as a secondary discovery tool, not the primary one

 

So while hashtags still matter, they don’t carry your content anymore. Your content has to do that on its own.

 

You can also use an Instagram growth service to help get more reach, and you should still make sure your content is top notch for real results in the long run. 

Are Instagram Hashtags Still Relevant in 2026?

Yes, but they’re no longer the star of the show. Instagram has shifted toward AI-driven content discovery and new algorithm functionality. 

 

Instead of relying heavily on hashtags, the platform now reads your captions, analyzes your video content, and tracks how users interact with your posts.

 

This means Instagram is asking:

  • Are people watching your video all the way through?
  • Are they saving or sharing your post?
  • Does your content match what users are searching for?

 

If the answer is yes, your content gets pushed further.

 

Hashtags now play a smaller role. They help with context, but they won’t fix weak content or low engagement.

 

Think of hashtags like seasoning. They enhance the meal, but it’s not the main ingredient. 

Keywords are King

If there’s one big shift you need to understand, it’s this: keywords matter more than hashtags now.

 

Instagram is basically becoming a search engine. People type things like “how to grow on Instagram” or “best coffee in LA,” and the platform shows content based on keywords, not just hashtags.

 

That means you should focus on:

  • Writing captions that include searchable phrases
  • Using clear, descriptive language in your posts
  • Saying keywords out loud in your videos
  • Adding keywords to your bio and name field

 

For example, instead of just writing a vague caption, say exactly what your content is about:

“Here are 3 Instagram growth tips for small business owners in 2026.”

 

That tells Instagram exactly who to show your post to. Hashtags can support this, but keywords do the heavy lifting.

 

It’s also very common for captions to use Instagram emojis so that they are more personable and descriptive. They won’t usually be your hashtags, but they should be in your content somewhere to follow the trends.

When You Should Still Use Instagram Hashtags

Even though hashtags aren’t the main growth tool anymore, there are still specific situations where they can give you a real advantage. The key is using them with intention instead of just adding them out of habit.

1. Running Campaigns

Hashtags are still very effective when you’re running a campaign because they create a clear, searchable thread of content.

 

If you’re launching a product, hosting a giveaway, or doing a limited-time challenge, a branded hashtag helps organize everything in one place. It also makes it easier for your audience to participate and follow along.

 

#shareacoke was an incredibly popular and viral campaign that took advantage of hashtags, and it’s still popular on the platform today. 

 

For example, if you’re doing a 7-day challenge, using a hashtag like #7DaysWith[YourBrand] lets people click the tag and instantly see all posts related to that campaign.

 

This builds momentum and makes your campaign feel bigger than just a single post.

 

It also helps you track results. You can quickly see who is մասնակցating, what content they’re creating, and how your campaign is spreading across Instagram.

2. User-Generated Content (UGC)

Hashtags are one of the easiest ways to collect content from your audience without extra tools.

 

When you ask your customers or followers to post using a specific hashtag, you create a stream of real content tied to your brand. 

 

This is powerful because people trust content from other users more than polished brand posts.

 

For example, a skincare brand might ask customers to use #GlowWith[BrandName]. Over time, that hashtag becomes a library of testimonials, before-and-after results, and real experiences.

 

This does two things. First, it gives you free content you can repost. Second, it builds credibility because new customers can see real people using your product.

 

To make this work, you need to remind your audience often. Mention the hashtag in your captions, stories, and even your bio so people actually use it.

 

You can get more Instagram followers when you engage followers through their own content. 

3. Community Building

Hashtags still play a role in helping you connect with niche communities, especially if you’re in a specific industry or interest group.

 

Instead of trying to reach everyone, hashtags can help you reach the right people. When you use smaller, targeted hashtags, your content is more likely to show up in front of users who already care about that topic.

 

For example, if you’re a fitness coach for beginners, hashtags like #BeginnerFitnessJourney or #HomeWorkoutTips can connect you with people who are just starting out. 

 

Here you can see an example of hashtags specifically for women workout content. 

 

These users are more likely to engage, follow, and eventually become customers. 

 

Hashtags also help you engage back. You can click on those same hashtags, find posts from your target audience, and interact with them. 

 

This creates a two-way relationship instead of just posting and hoping people find you. 

 

Over time, this builds a sense of community around your content, which is much more valuable than random reach.

You can also buy Instagram likes to help boost your engagement numbers and generate more natural engagement from your community over time.

Best Practices for Instagram Hashtags

If you’re going to use hashtags, you need to use them smartly. The old “just add 30 random hashtags” strategy doesn’t work anymore.

 

Here are five practical tips to help you get reach:

 

First, focus on relevance. Your hashtags should match your content exactly. If your post is about Instagram growth, don’t throw in unrelated trending hashtags just to chase reach. Instagram can tell.

 

Second, use fewer hashtags. You don’t need 30 anymore. In fact, Instagram now only allows 0-5 hashtags per post, a limit they are rolling out slowly to all reels and posts. So, they want you to be more targeted and focused instead of using a bunch of tags. 

 

Third, mix niche and mid-size hashtags. Avoid super broad hashtags with millions of posts. Your content will get buried. Instead, use hashtags that your ideal audience is actually browsing.

 

Fourth, treat hashtags as support, not strategy. Your hook, visuals, and message matter more than anything else. If your content isn’t engaging, hashtags won’t save it.

 

Fifth, test and adjust. Instagram is always changing. Try different hashtag sets and see what works best for your audience.

 

If you need help thinking of good hashtags, you can use an Instagram hashtag generator for ideas, or do some market research and see what competitors are doing.  

Final verdict: Are Instagram Hashtags Dead?

No, Instagram hashtags are not dead. But they are no longer the main way to grow.

 

In 2026, growth comes from:

  • Strong content that holds attention
  • Clear messaging with keywords
  • High engagement like saves and shares
  • Consistency over time

 

Hashtags are just one small piece of the puzzle now.

 

If you rely only on hashtags, your growth will likely stall. But if you combine good content, smart keywords, and strategic hashtags, you can still grow your reach and build your brand.

The game has changed, but the opportunity is still there.

FAQ

Are hashtags pointless now?

Not pointless, just less powerful.

 

Hashtags still help categorize your content and can bring in small amounts of extra reach. But they won’t drive massive growth on their own anymore. 

 

You need strong content and keyword optimization to see real results.

What is the 333 hashtag rule?

The 333 hashtag rule is a strategy where you rotate between three sets of 3 hashtags, often mixing niche, medium, and broad tags.

 

While this method used to work better in the past, it’s not as important today. What matters more is choosing hashtags that are highly relevant to your content and audience.

What’s replacing hashtags?

Keywords and content signals are replacing hashtags.

Instagram now focuses on what your content is about, how people interact with it, and whether it matches user searches. 

 

That means your Instagram captions, video text, and spoken words matter more than hashtags.

Does Gen Z use hashtags?

Yes, but differently.

 

Gen Z tends to use fewer hashtags and focuses more on captions, trends, and relatable content. They care more about authenticity and entertainment than optimizing posts with a long list of hashtags.

 

If you’re targeting Gen Z, focus on content quality first. Hashtags should be minimal and intentional.

How to Become Famous on YouTube Gaming?

You intend to start a YouTube channel to get famous! But, how do you get your gaming videos and tutorials on YouTube popular? Creating content isn’t enough; you have to put more effort into getting your content popular and acquiring enough fame and audience base for your gaming channel on YouTube. One effective strategy could be to buy YouTube likes. This approach can help boost your videos’ credibility and visibility, allowing you to reach a broader audience and increase engagement with your content.

Here is a list of simple steps you need to practice with consistency to see real results.

#1. Promote your videos

After you have streamlined your gaming videos into the calendar and started uploading, it’s time you start the promotions.

Leverage the publicity of your other social media channels like Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, and more to share your gaming videos. You can also reach out to other websites and get the word out about the channel.

The better the visibility of your YouTube channel, the more you are inclined to gain new subscribers and viewers.

#2. Be patient

Patience is the key to getting popular on YouTube. You won’t see results overnight; however, it shouldn’t stop you from making more content. In time, if you are consistent with ideating, planning, and promoting your videos on YouTube, you will start to see evident growth in the follower count on the media. Remember what you are here for, and give yourself a time bracket wherein you will only put in work and not expect anything out of it! For those looking for a strategic boost, YouTube growth services can offer valuable support in expanding your reach and building a loyal audience.

#3. Collaborate with other YouTubers

Collaborating with fellow creators is one of the proven tips for growing a Brand New YouTube Channel. Therefore, you need to start putting out collaboration requests to other gaming YouTubers on YouTube.

Find them through email, direct messages (DM), and other ways, and check out if they are interested in collaborating with you. You will get many ‘NOs,’ but you need to keep looking and sending requests. You will surely find a Yes!

YouTube collaborations help you with better exposure to new audiences and a lot of fun while creating content!

#4. Create interesting thumbnails

YouTube thumbnail is the first one any viewer on YouTube notices about your channel. Chances are that 85% of viewers would click on your video if the Thumbnail excites them about the content.

Use a clickbait title, bright colors, and attention-seeking images that attract people and compel them to watch your videos.

#5. Use keywords in your titles and descriptions

If you don’t use the right keywords and tags on your videos, your target audience searching for similar content you create won’t be able to reach your gaming videos and tutorials.

You can include relevant tags and keywords on your video titles, and descriptions so that it comes on top of the search results. This will, eventually, lead to getting more views on your gaming videos and, thus, attract new subscribers on YouTube.

When you consistently use keywords on your YouTube gaming videos, your content will start ranking and appearing on top in time after consistent use!

How To View Private Instagram Profile in 2025: 4 Basic Ways

If you’re wondering why and how to view Private Instagram Accounts Instantly, there can be one or many reasons to view a private profile. Many parents want to keep an eye on what their kids are doing, or there is a need to break into your friend’s profile to find some information. Additionally, knowing how to share Instagram profile information securely can help you share profiles with others without compromising privacy. the below-mentioned tops are for your rescue.  

#1. Send A Follow Request

The straightforward way of viewing someone’s private profile is by sending them a follow request on Instagram. This old-school method will 100 percent work, and you will also have the account holder’s permission to view their content. 

After they approve your follow request, you can see all the posts they have updated on their profile and future ones. Furthermore, you will abide by the platform’s guidelines to see someone’s content on their private profile. 

However, one challenge with this method is the willingness of the account holder. Having said that, there is no guarantee that they will accept your request, and that you will have access to their content. 

#2. Explore Mutual Connections

Another way to break through into someone’s private Instagram account is a bit sneaky. You must find a friend or a mutual connection who is already following that user. It involves a high level of trust in the mutual person that they will not leak it to the private account owner. 

You will also have to check that it’s fine with your friends and that they will allow you to sneak into someone’s account through theirs. With this method, you can view a private Instagram account without following them or making them aware. 

You might encounter some challenges during the process and will not have permanent access to their account. 

#3. Search for Public Posts

Another sneaky method of viewing a private account on Instagram is by searching for their handle on Google. Google search is free, and you can anonymously access some of their posts by chance. Type in the user’s handle on Google and browse through the pictures you get. 

You may find some public posts from their account, pictures they were tagged in, or pictures their friends posted. Additionally, searching through Google is less invasive and doesn’t violate any rules. 

The tip of Google search is more like a shot in the dark. You may obtain expected results or may end up with nothing. 

#4. Consider Third Party Apps (with Caution)

If you want to view a private Instagram account, you can search for them on other platforms. They may have some content posted on other social media platforms on their profile. You can peep into their profile on other platforms instead of doing it on Instagram. 

Nowadays, many people have their Instagram accounts synced with Facebook, and every content published on one platform gets posted on another. The user whose profile you want to see should have a presence on other platforms, too. 

By stalking their profile on other platforms, you can simply stay updated with their content anonymously.

Key Takeaway

  • TikTok’s algorithm pushes videos based on engagement, not follower count. Even new users with zero followers can go viral if the content hits the mark.

  • The first 2–3 seconds of your video must be attention-grabbing to stop users from scrolling. A solid hook increases your chances of higher watch time and shares.

  • Posting 1–3 times daily improves your chances of going viral. Don’t wait for the perfect video—stay active and authentic.

  • Good lighting, clear visuals, and engaging editing enhance viewer retention and rewatchability, which the TikTok algorithm favors.

  • Good lighting, clear visuals, and engaging editing enhance viewer retention and rewatchability, which the TikTok algorithm favors.

title: 15 Ways To Grow Your Fashion Instagram Account
description: Fashion influencers and brands are increasingly moving their businesses to social media. Unsurprisingly, there are 4.74 million users worldwide (59.4% of…
excerpt: Fashion influencers and brands are increasingly moving their businesses to social media. Unsurprisingly, there are 4.74 million users worldwide (59.4% of the entire global population). Facebook and…
author: Elizabeth Ray
heroAlt: 15 Ways To Grow Your Fashion Instagram Account — Instagram guide on Thunderclap

blocks.html:
Fashion influencers and brands are increasingly moving their businesses to social media. Unsurprisingly, there are 4.74 million users worldwide (59.4% of the entire global population). Facebook and YouTube are the largest social media sites, with 3 billion and 2.5 billion users, respectively.

However, with 2 billion monthly active users, Instagram is catching up and is tied with WhatsApp for the third most prominent social media app. If you’re a fashion brand or influencer looking to grow your fashion Instagram account this year while monetizing your profile, read on for some great tips. 

 

This blog offers 15 practical ways to grow your fashion Instagram account, helping you stand out in a competitive market and attract a dedicated following of fashion enthusiasts.

How To Start A Fashion Instagram Account?

Starting a fashion Instagram account is a wonderful effort that allows you to communicate your sense of style and fashion knowledge to a worldwide audience.  Here’s a guide to help you grow your fashion Instagram account.

 

1. Define Your Niche 

Identify what makes your sense of style unique. A particular niche can help you draw in a specific type of customer, whether premium labels, high street clothes, vintage apparel, or sustainable fashion. 

2. Create an Appealing Profile 

Select a catchy username that accurately reflects your business. A trendy headshot or a logo would provide excellent examples of a professional and brand-consistent profile photo. 

Compose an engaging bio that highlights the features of your account and incorporates a call-to-action (CTA), such as a URL to your fashion website or blog.

3. Create a Content Plan 

Plan your content around your niche. High-quality images are essential, so buy a decent camera or smartphone and pick up some basic editing and shooting techniques. 

To keep your account relevant, switch your content with videos, behind-the-scenes photos, outfit posts, and fashion advice. Create and stick to a posting schedule since consistency is essential. 

4. Engage Your Audience 

On Instagram, interaction is essential, so participate in discussions within the fashion community, answer comments on your postings, and interact with what your followers have to offer. Use live sessions and Instagram Stories to interact with your audience in real time.

5. Make Use of Geotags and Hashtags 

Make use of appropriate hashtags to improve your content’ discoverability. Look for trending hashtags related to fashion and use general and industry-specific ones. You may also draw in local companies and followers by geotagging your posts.

title: What Is A Good Engagement Rate On Instagram? 14 Best Tips
description: Instagram is an effective strategy to use for business and in today’s world most creators know this. Boasting one of the highest engagement rates across…
excerpt: Instagram is an effective strategy to use for business and in today’s world most creators know this. Boasting one of the highest engagement rates across any social media marketing channel, Instagram…
author: Elizabeth Ray
heroAlt: What Is A Good Engagement Rate On Instagram? 14 Best Tips — Instagram guide on Thunderclap

blocks.html:
Instagram is an effective strategy to use for business and in today’s world most creators know this. Boasting one of the highest engagement rates across any social media marketing channel, Instagram is one of the top choices for marketers that are trying to grow their business or brand.

With over a billion users on Instagram every single month, the platform is primed and ready to deliver a return on investment (if done correctly). That’s over 1/7th of the world’s population!

 

 

 

When Instagram launched, it was more about how many followers you had on your profile. Nowadays, buying Instagram followers has become a vital part of your strategy to reach a wider audience and gain credibility. However, not just any followers will do. It’s important to focus on building an engaged community that truly cares about your brand.

When Instagram launched, it was more about how many followers you had on your profile.

But as the industry standards grew, most businesses and brands put the follower count on the backburner and focused on another metric…

That metric is engagement. If you want your Instagram account to yield any kind of ROI, it’s completely understandable to care more about your engagement rate and less about your follower count.

Like Hootsuite says, “engagement only have value when it’s genuine, AKA, coming from real people and profiles.”

In order to reach even more users, your content needs to connect you with your current audience in a way that makes them want to engage with it.

By engage, I’m talking about commenting on your posts, sharing them, liking them, saving them, following your account, mentioning your account, using branded hashtags, click throughs on your links, and DMs.

All these engagement “signals” tell Instagram that your account is in demand. This, in turn, gives your content and account a higher “trust” score within the IG algorithm.

Because of this, you’ll start to notice even more engagement and reach from things like reaching the explore page or being higher in the Instagram newsfeed.

At the end of the day, getting better engagement isn’t easy. The good news is that it’s a straightforward task, but you have to be committed to it.

Read on if you want to know what Instagram classifies as a good engagement rate and how to increase your engagement rate by implementing these things right away!

Let’s first talk about what engagement rate is not. If you think your follower count is an engagement rate, then you’re wrong.

Instagram engagement rates are metrics of interactions that your fan base makes on the content you create and upload to Instagram.

Specifically, engagement metrics are measure by the following:

  • Comments
  • Shares
  • Likes
  • Saves
  • Followers & Growth Trajectory
  • Mentions
  • Branded Hashtags
  • Click-throughs
  • DMs

When real people take these kind of actions, it signals Instagram that your Instagram posts matter to their community which gives your account more “weight”, or “trust”.

In other words, it means that your target audience is digging what you’re creating. This is powerful because it means that you have influence within your fan base and will increase your number of followers.

A piece of content SO good, that you just KNEW everybody was going to absolutely LOVE it. And because of this, you thought that you’d have an avalanche of engagement and new followers just sliding in…

But when you actually post your content, life slaps you across the face with a big fat L sign. You’re hurt, your soul is ripped into shreds, and your ego is deflated (like Tom Brady’s Super Bowl deflategate). In all seriousness, engagement (or lack of) all comes down to understanding the best times to post on Instagram.

Timing is crucial when it comes to content visibility. The Instagram algorithm tends to prioritize content that receives quick engagement, so posting when your audience is most active is key. But if you’re still struggling to build that momentum, one effective way to gain traction is by buying Instagram followers. Increasing your follower count can give your profile a credibility boost, which may encourage organic engagement from real users and increase the chances of your content showing up on more feeds and in the Explore section.

By combining strategic posting times with a solid follower base, you’re giving your content the best shot at reaching a wider audience and engaging more followers.

Who’s online when you post your content is so critical because immediate engagement signals to Instagram that, hey… this is content people actually want to see. It’s a big ranking factor when it comes to prioritizing your content in the newsfeed. When you look into this at a deeper level, you start to realize that you need to know the best time to post for your specific Instagram account category, AKA niche.

You want your content to come out at the moment like that when your target audience has its full attention on Instagram, not when people are working, resting, spending time with friends and family, or offline. View this post on Instagram. A post shared by Cristiano Ronaldo (@cristiano) And while we can all agree that Cristiano can post whenever he damn well pleases and still yield mind-boggling amounts of engagement…

I can promise you that he still sticks to a time he KNOWS he’ll get the most amount of eyeballs on his content. It’s true that your content will be seen eventually (if the algorithm permits). However, knowing the best time to post on Instagram can be extremely beneficial for achieving optimal engagement and ensuring the maximum amount of exposure.

It also helps you to keep things organized and create a plan that you can actually stick to! At this point, you’re probably like, “damn, when is the best time for ME to post…“ Well, if you search online, you can clearly see that there are about a million and a half different answers. After all, many factors could play into it. To make things easier for you, we’ll discuss a wide range of answers to this question.

This will help you to have a more complete understanding of the best time to post on Instagram and why there are so many different answers in the first place.  Opinions are like a$(ho$)s — everybody has one.
